language: C# (SMA AlgorithmsUtils LPCreation abstraction over Gurobi)

models.description: SPOT (a Periodic Event Scheduling Problem / PESP extension after Polinder, Schmidt & Huisman 2021) for strategic passenger railway timetabling. The model fixes, per period of length T (CycleTime), the arrival and departure event times of each train at each stop so as to minimise total demand-weighted passenger travel time. Passengers choose exactly one of several candidate travel routes per OD relation; each route is a sequence of in-train route parts joined by transfers. Periodic (cyclic, modulo-T) timing relations between successive events are linearised with a cycle-time-shift variable k (the integer/binary wrap variable) so that running-time, stop-time, route-part-duration and transfer relations hold within one period. This implementation deliberately deviates from the original SPOT (readme.md:54-84): it neglects waiting time at the passenger origin and assumes all passengers of an OD relation use the same route (single route selection per relation). Build is orchestrated by SpotProblemGenerationCommand -> {AddObjectiveCommand, AddTrainRunConstraintsCommand, AddRelationsConstraintsCommand}. The model is assembled via SMA's AlgorithmsUtils LPCreation abstraction (Constraint/Term/Monomial/ContinuousVariable/BinaryVariable/Objective), not raw gurobipy/GRBModel; that library wraps Gurobi (readme.md:221-247).

sets_indices.name: L
sets_indices.meaning: set of (template) train lines in the scenario

sets_indices.name: P_l
sets_indices.meaning: ordered sequence of path-node constraints (events) of line l

sets_indices.name: R
sets_indices.meaning: set of passenger OD relations that have at least one routing

sets_indices.name: TR_r
sets_indices.meaning: candidate travel routes for relation r

sets_indices.name: RP_{r,tr}
sets_indices.meaning: ordered in-train route parts of travel route tr

sets_indices.name: TRX_{r,tr}
sets_indices.meaning: transfers between consecutive route parts of travel route tr (consecutive pairs of route parts)

sets_indices.name: VN_{rp}
sets_indices.meaning: visited path nodes (events) along a route part rp

parameters.name: T
parameters.meaning: cycle time / period of the PESP (length of one period); used as the modulo base in every periodic timing constraint

parameters.name: TimeStep
parameters.meaning: time discretisation granularity = 6 seconds; ToModelTime divides real durations by this to get integer model time

parameters.name: l^stop_n
parameters.meaning: minimum stop (dwell) time required at path node n

parameters.name: l^run_n
parameters.meaning: minimum running (travel) time on the section ending at node n (predecessor -> n)

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.meaning: additional-running-time factor; bounds slack running time as floor(alpha * l^run_n)

parameters.name: l^trans_{a,d}
parameters.meaning: required minimum transfer (connection) time between an arriving and a departing event at a transfer

parameters.name: phi_r
parameters.meaning: total passenger demand of OD relation r (objective weight)

parameters.name: TransferMax
parameters.meaning: maximum transfer time, used as upper bound on transfer-duration variables and in big-M route-duration bounds

parameters.name: M_{r,tr}
parameters.meaning: big-M for the route-selection linking lower bound = maximum possible total travel time on route tr (CalculateMaximumTravelTimeOnTravelRoute)

decision_variables.name: dep_{l,n}
decision_variables.meaning: departure event time of line l at path node n, within one period

decision_variables.name: arr_{l,n}
decision_variables.meaning: arrival event time of line l at path node n, within one period

decision_variables.name: k_{i,j}
decision_variables.meaning: cycle-time-shift / modulo wrap variable for the periodic relation between events i and j: number of periods T added so the linearised cyclic constraint holds (a single period wrap, modelled as binary)

decision_variables.name: addrun_{l,n}
decision_variables.meaning: additional (slack) running time beyond the minimum on the section ending at node n

decision_variables.name: rpd_{rp}
decision_variables.meaning: duration spent on in-train route part rp

decision_variables.name: ptd_{x}
decision_variables.meaning: transfer duration at transfer x between two consecutive route parts

decision_variables.name: trd_{tr}
decision_variables.meaning: total travel duration on travel route tr (sum of route-part durations + transfer durations)

decision_variables.name: srd_r
decision_variables.meaning: selected (minimal/realised) total travel duration for relation r; objective term

decision_variables.name: rsd_{tr}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if travel route tr is the route selected for its relation

objective.expression_plain: minimise total demand-weighted passenger travel time: sum over OD relations of (total demand of the relation times its selected total travel duration)

constraints.name: stop_time_with_dwell
constraints.indexed_over: l in L, n in P_l with l^stop_n > 0
constraints.expression_plain: departure at a commercial stop is at least arrival plus minimum dwell time, taken modulo the period T via cycle-time-shift k (classic PESP stop-duration constraint) (C02a_STOP)

constraints.name: stop_time_without_dwell
constraints.indexed_over: l in L, n in P_l with l^stop_n = 0
constraints.expression_plain: at non-stop path nodes the departure event equals the arrival event (no dwell) (C02b_STOP)

constraints.name: running_time_equality
constraints.indexed_over: l in L, n in P_l \ {first node} (predecessor = n.Predecessor)
constraints.expression_latex: arr_{l,n} = dep_{l,n^-} + l^{run}_n + addrun_{l,n} - T \cdot k_{n^-,n}
constraints.expression_plain: arrival at a node equals departure at its predecessor plus the minimum running time plus chosen additional running time, taken modulo T via cycle-time-shift k (PESP running-time / travel-time constraint) (C01_RUN)

constraints.name: additional_running_time_bound
constraints.indexed_over: l in L, n in P_l \ {first node}
constraints.expression_plain: additional (slack) running time on a section is capped at floor(alpha * minimum running time) (C10_ADDR)

constraints.name: route_part_duration_def
constraints.indexed_over: r in R, tr in TR_r, rp in RP_{r,tr}
constraints.expression_latex: rpd_{rp} = (arr^{end}_{rp} - dep^{start}_{rp}) + T \sum_{i \in (interior\ stops)\cup(running\ segments)} k_i
constraints.expression_plain: duration on a route part equals end-arrival minus start-departure event times, plus T times the sum of all cycle-time-shift variables of the interior stop events and of the running segments along the part (re-adds the period wraps subtracted in the per-event PESP constraints so the route-part duration is a true elapsed time) (C03_TRPD)

constraints.name: transfer_duration_def
constraints.indexed_over: r in R, tr in TR_r, x in TRX_{r,tr}
constraints.expression_plain: transfer duration equals departing-event time of the next route part minus arriving-event time of the previous route part, taken modulo T via cycle-time-shift k (PESP transfer/connection timing) (C09_RPTD)

constraints.name: required_minimum_transfer_duration
constraints.indexed_over: r in R, tr in TR_r, x in TRX_{r,tr}
constraints.expression_plain: the transfer duration at each transfer must be at least the required minimum transfer time for that arriving/departing event pair (C08_RPTM)

constraints.name: travel_route_total_duration
constraints.indexed_over: r in R, tr in TR_r
constraints.expression_plain: total duration of a travel route equals the sum of its route-part durations plus the sum of its transfer durations (C07_TRD)

constraints.name: selected_duration_upper_bound
constraints.indexed_over: r in R, tr in TR_r
constraints.expression_plain: the relation's selected travel duration is at most every candidate route's total duration, i.e. it is bounded above by the minimum over routes (C04_MIND). Class is named ...UpperBoundForMinimum... ; the name-factory tag is C04_MIND.

constraints.name: selected_duration_lower_bound_linking
constraints.indexed_over: r in R, tr in TR_r
constraints.expression_plain: if route tr is selected (rsd_tr = 1) then srd_r >= trd_tr, forcing the selected duration up to the chosen route's duration; if not selected the big-M term deactivates the bound. Together with the upper bound this fixes srd_r to the duration of the selected route (C05_MAXD). Class is named ...LowerBoundForMinimum... ; the name-factory tag is C05_MAXD.

constraints.name: select_exactly_one_route
constraints.expression_plain: exactly one candidate travel route is selected per OD relation (all passengers of the relation use the same route, per the implementation's simplification) (C06_TRDS)

models.notes: PESP modulo/period representation: the period T is scenario.CycleTime converted to integer model-time units (6 s steps) by TimeConverter.ToModelTime (Spot/Model/Scenario/TimeConverter.cs:24-26). Every cyclic timing relation (stop C02a, running C01, transfer C09) is linearised as event_j - event_i - offset = -T*k or >= ..., where k = CreateCycleTimeShift is the wrap variable. IMPORTANT FIDELITY NOTE: in this code k is declared as a BinaryVariable (SpotVariableFactory.cs:40-48, returns new BinaryVariable), i.e. at most one period wrap per relation, NOT a general non-negative integer modulo variable as in textbook PESP. The route-part-duration constraint (C03) re-adds T*k for the interior events to recover true elapsed durations. The minimum-over-routes selection (C04 upper bound + C05 big-M lower bound + C06 select-one) realises srd_r = duration of the selected route. NAMING CAVEAT: the C# command class names for the two selection bounds are swapped relative to the constraint-name-factory tags - AddTravelRouteDurationUpperBoundForMinimumConstraintCommand emits tag C04_MIND (the <= bound) and AddTravelRouteDurationLowerBoundForMinimumConstraintCommand emits tag C05_MAXD (the big-M >= bound); both are transcribed faithfully above. The model is built through SMA's AlgorithmsUtils LPCreation abstraction (Constraint, Term, Monomial, ContinuousVariable, BinaryVariable, Objective, ConstraintType.Geq/Leq/Eq, ObjectiveType.Min) which wraps Gurobi; the GRBModel/AddVar/AddConstr Gurobi calls themselves live in the external (not-vendored) AlgorithmsUtils library, so no raw Gurobi API lines appear in this repo. Variable bounds are set on construction in SpotVariableFactory (arrival/departure in [0, T - TimeStep]; rpd/trd/srd carry computed lower/upper bounds; ptd in [0, TransferMax]).

extraction_notes: Single MILP formulation; no second distinct model in the repo. Source is C# using SMA AlgorithmsUtils LPCreation abstraction over Gurobi (not gurobipy/GRBModel directly), confirmed in readme.md:221-247 ('AlgorithmsUtils library ... builds an abstraction API around the Gurobi MIP solver'). The full model is generated under Spot/MilpGeneration/, orchestrated by SpotProblemGenerationCommand -> AddObjectiveCommand + AddTrainRunConstraintsCommand (-> stop-time, running-time, additional-running-time-bound) + AddRelationsConstraintsCommand (per relation: route-part durations, transfer durations, total route duration, minimum-over-routes selection, required minimum transfer). 10 distinct constraint families (C01-C10) plus the objective, all with real source line refs. paper_reference DOI 10.1016/j.trb.2021.02.006 is the cited SPOT paper (Polinder, Schmidt, Huisman 2021, Transportation Research Part B), taken verbatim from readme.md:251-252 - VERIFIED from README, not inferred. instances: EMPTY/null - no datasets, scenario files, or .lp/.mps instances are vendored in the repo (input data is fetched at runtime from the Viriato Algorithm Platform REST interface per readme.md:14-16); App.config only holds platform connection settings. Parameter domains are stated in model-time units (integer multiples of the 6 s TimeStep). The cycle-time-shift wrap variable k is BINARY in this implementation (SpotVariableFactory.cs:40-48), a deliberate single-wrap simplification of the general PESP integer modulo variable - flagged in model notes.
